cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user provided a passage about flea markets and garage sales in America, and the question is about what we know about garage sales. The options are A to D. First, I need to read through the passage again to find the relevant information. The paragraph about garage sales starts with "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture." So it says they're part of American culture, which might relate to popularity. Then it mentions they're held in spring and fall when the weather is good. The next part says the homeowner is clearing out accumulated items that are no longer useful. The goods are in good condition and great buys can be found with time and patience. It also mentions that if the owner is selling the house, they might give things away below true worth. Looking at the options: A) Only held in spring. The passage says "usually in spring and fall". So "only" is incorrect. A is wrong. B) Goods are totally useless. The passage says the items are no longer useful to the owner, but the goods on display are in good condition. So B is incorrect because "totally useless" contradicts the text. C) Popular with Americans. Since the passage states that garage sales are "very much a part of American culture" and that people go to them to find great buys, this suggests they are popular. So C seems correct. D) All goods are selling far below real value. The passage mentions that if the owner is moving, they might have to give things away at a price far below true worth. But
